title: lycopodium repens
intro: Lycopodium repens, also known as running club moss or stag's horn fern, is a type of small evergreen fern that grows in dense, low-lying mats in woodland areas and wetlands. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of Lycopodium repens include its ability to ease symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Lycopodium repens can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or powder for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically proven.
